calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Apr-08-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Daphne Lin appeared by telephone for Lillian Howan (Executor).

Ms. Lin addressed the Court's concerns stating that the last time she had communication with the IRS Lien Office in Florida was on February 6th, that they indicated the case was going to be transferred to the San Jose branch, that they expected to be ready to record the lien in four to six weeks, and that due to cuts and staff shortages she does not believe the lien will be recorded anytime soon. Ms. Lin also stated that she does not believe there is a need to hold this up because the IRS is in the process of recording the lien, addressed the issue of fees stating that there was a clerical error, that they made a mistake when doing calculations of the statutory fee, that initially the agreement to reduce their fees was based on the miscalculated statutory fee, that they made a correction and that they discussed it with their Client. In addition, Ms. Lin stated that there is an agreement on a fee amount of $95,000.00 which is less than the statutory fee amount. The Court noted that the proposed order does not address fees. The Court granted the Petition for Final Distribution, etc. (subject to receiving an order with the attorney fee at $95,000.00 given that it is less than the statutory fee amount).

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Chan. Not reported.

Off Calendar May-23-2018 Continued To Jul-18-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Daphne Lin for Executor via CourtCall

Ms. Lin requested a 60-day continuance, stating that they are resolving estate tax issues with IRS. The status on close of estate is continued to July 18, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for filing of a petition for final distribution or a petition for approval of report on status of administration.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Shirley Le. Deputy: V. Jung. Not reported.
